prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(select_animal_for_processing source_group_1)
(assign_load_slot_to_animal source_group_1 load_slot_1)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(confirm_weighing_and_mark_ready source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(provision_consumable_to_group source_group_1 loading_zone_a_1 consumable_feed_1)
(finalize_group_preparation_zone_a source_group_1 loading_zone_a_1 weighing_station_1 consumable_feed_1)
(release_weighing_station source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(select_animal_for_processing receiving_group_1)
(assign_load_slot_to_animal receiving_group_1 load_slot_2)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal receiving_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(confirm_weighing_and_mark_ready receiving_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(inspect_loading_zone_b_for_receiving_group receiving_group_1 loading_zone_b_1 weighing_station_1)
(assign_handler_to_entity receiving_group_1 stock_handler_1)
(confirm_receiving_group_handler_and_mark_prepared receiving_group_1 loading_zone_b_1 stock_handler_1)
(release_weighing_station receiving_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(assign_vehicle_to_groups_and_link_zones source_group_1 receiving_group_1 loading_zone_a_1 loading_zone_b_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(mark_vehicle_ready_for_loading transport_vehicle_1 source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(release_weighing_station source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(confirm_source_group_release source_group_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(confirm_receiving_group_release receiving_group_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(select_animal_for_processing handling_station_1)
(assign_load_slot_to_animal handling_station_1 load_slot_3)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal handling_station_1 weighing_station_1)
(confirm_weighing_and_mark_ready handling_station_1 weighing_station_1)
(allocate_container_to_station_and_attach_to_vehicle handling_station_1 cargo_container_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(prime_station_with_allocated_container handling_station_1 cargo_container_1 transport_vehicle_1 weighing_station_1)
(release_handler_from_entity receiving_group_1 stock_handler_1)
(assign_handler_to_entity handling_station_1 stock_handler_1)
(attach_veterinary_kit_to_station handling_station_1 veterinary_kit_1)
(perform_veterinary_inspection_at_station handling_station_1 veterinary_kit_1 stock_handler_1 cargo_container_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(attach_biosecurity_kit_to_station handling_station_1 biosecurity_kit_1)
(perform_biosecurity_inspection_and_mark handling_station_1 biosecurity_kit_1 cargo_container_1 transport_vehicle_1)
(finalize_station_checks_and_mark_ready handling_station_1)
(assign_identification_tag_to_entity handling_station_1 identification_tag_1 weighing_station_1)
(authorize_station_for_transport handling_station_1 load_slot_3 identification_tag_1)
(detach_veterinary_kit_from_station handling_station_1 veterinary_kit_1)
(detach_biosecurity_kit_from_station handling_station_1 biosecurity_kit_1)
(release_handler_from_entity handling_station_1 stock_handler_1)
(release_weighing_station handling_station_1 weighing_station_1)
(select_animal_for_processing handling_station_2)
(assign_load_slot_to_animal handling_station_2 load_slot_3)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal handling_station_2 weighing_station_1)
(confirm_weighing_and_mark_ready handling_station_2 weighing_station_1)
(allocate_container_to_station_and_attach_to_vehicle handling_station_2 cargo_container_2 transport_vehicle_1)
(prime_station_with_allocated_container handling_station_2 cargo_container_2 transport_vehicle_1 weighing_station_1)
(assign_handler_to_entity handling_station_2 stock_handler_1)
(attach_veterinary_kit_to_station handling_station_2 veterinary_kit_1)
(perform_veterinary_inspection_at_station handling_station_2 veterinary_kit_1 stock_handler_1 cargo_container_2 transport_vehicle_1)
(attach_biosecurity_kit_to_station handling_station_2 biosecurity_kit_1)
(perform_biosecurity_inspection_and_mark handling_station_2 biosecurity_kit_1 cargo_container_2 transport_vehicle_1)
(finalize_station_checks_and_mark_ready handling_station_2)
(assign_identification_tag_to_entity handling_station_2 identification_tag_1 weighing_station_1)
(authorize_station_for_transport handling_station_2 load_slot_3 identification_tag_1)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal source_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(assign_identification_tag_to_entity source_group_1 identification_tag_1 weighing_station_1)
(authorize_group_for_transport source_group_1 load_slot_1 identification_tag_1)
(reserve_weighing_station_for_animal receiving_group_1 weighing_station_1)
(assign_identification_tag_to_entity receiving_group_1 identification_tag_1 weighing_station_1)
(authorize_receiving_group_for_transport receiving_group_1 load_slot_2 identification_tag_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for livestock transport readiness. I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve it, each on its own line in the form (action-name ?args).


## Understanding the Task  
- The problem involves creating a planning solution in PDDL for livestock transport readiness.  
- The output must list ground actions in sequence, each on a new line, formatted as (action-name ?args).  

## Defining the Output  
- Focus is on generating a series of specific actions to solve the planning problem.  
- Each action should include the action name and its arguments, following the given format.

## Identifying Initial Conditions  
- Handling_station_1 has inspection_certificate_1, and handling_station_2 has inspection_certificate_2, but certificate_2 isn’t initially available.  
- A biosecurity kit is available from the start, while no entities are selected or ready for processing initially.  

## Analyzing Entity Types  
- Source_group_1, receiving_group_1, handling_station_1, and handling_station_2 are all identifiable_entities, including stations as station_group_class.  
- Handling_stations are treated specially in some actions, despite sharing entity processes like selection and processing.  

## Exploring Clearance Requirements  
- To achieve cleared_for_transport for entities, they need to be identified, assigned a load slot, and given an identification tag.  
- This process requires prior steps like releasing for loading, which depends on confirmation actions and readiness conditions for groups and stations.  

## Mapping Action Dependencies  
- Actions like authorize_group_for_transport and authorize_station_for_transport are specific to entity types, requiring readiness and identification.  
- The flow involves selecting entities, staging, assigning weighing stations, and ensuring handlers are assigned, with variations for groups and stations.

## Examining Station Readiness  
- Stations like handling_station_1 and handling_station_2 need vehicle configuration and readiness to allocate containers, involving actions like allocate_container_to_station_and_attach_to_vehicle.  
- Vehicle readiness depends on marking it ready, which requires source group handler confirmation and assigned weighing stations, creating a dependency loop.  

## Exploring Group Processing  
- Groups like source_group_1 and receiving_group_1 need to be processed first to ready the vehicle, involving handler confirmation and zone inspection.  
- Actions like provision_consumable_to_group are limited by one consumable, suggesting sequential processing for source and receiving groups.  

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- Only one handler and one consumable are available, requiring entities to be processed sequentially for readiness.  
- Load slots and weighing stations are also limited, with three slots and one station, affecting the order of operations.  

## Planning the Sequence  
- Start by selecting all entities (source_group_1, receiving_group_1, handling_station_1, handling_station_2) for processing and assigning load slots.  
- Process one group to ready, assign a handler, inspect the zone, and confirm handler prepared, then repeat for the next group before handling stations.

## Resolving Weighing Station Conflicts  
- After source group is ready and provisioned, the weighing station remains assigned, blocking receiving group processing.  
- Finalizing source group preparation requires the weighing station to stay assigned, creating a dependency conflict.  

## Sequencing Group Preparation  
- Finalize source group preparation first while the weighing station is still assigned, freeing the consumable afterward.  
- Release the weighing station after finalization, allowing the receiving group to reserve it for readiness and inspection.  

## Managing Handler Availability  
- The handler remains available after source group preparation, enabling assignment to the receiving group.  
- Confirm handler preparation for the receiving group after readiness, ensuring both groups are handler-confirmed and prepared.  

## Ensuring Zone Inspections  
- Inspecting receiving group’s zone requires the weighing station to be assigned, following the same pattern as source group finalization.  
- Both groups’ zones are inspected and prepared, with consumable availability restored after source group finalization.

## Preparing ID Tag Assignment  
- Each entity, including source and receiving groups, needs an ID tag assigned, using the single available identification_tag_1, which can be reused after authorization.  
- Assigning an ID tag requires the weighing station to be assigned, creating a sequential dependency for each entity.  

## Managing Station Readiness  
- Stations (handling_station_1, handling_station_2) need to be selected, assigned load slots, and staged, with load_slot_3 still available for assignment.  
- Processing stations requires them to be ready_for_processing, linked to the vehicle, and have containers allocated and attached, given vehicle_configured and vehicle_ready states.  

## Handling Station-Specific Steps  
- Priming stations involves allocating containers and attaching them, with options varying by vehicle sealing status (e.g., vehicle_sealed, permit_attached).  
- Veterinary checks and biosecurity processes follow, requiring station_primed status and handler assignment, with additional certifications needed for clearance.

## Releasing Resources for Efficiency  
- Release the handler from the receiving group after readiness, freeing it for station tasks like handling_station_1.  
- Release the weighing station from handling_station_1 once primed, enabling reassignment to handle_station_2.  

## Attaching Equipment for Inspections  
- Attach the veterinary kit to handling_station_1, making it ready for inspection with vet_kit_1 no longer available.  
- Attach the biosecurity kit to handling_station_1 after veterinary checks, ensuring station readiness for biosecurity processes.  

## Completing Station Inspections  
- Perform veterinary inspection at handling_station_1, completing it without requiring vehicle inspection, marking vet_completed.  
- Conduct biosecurity inspection at handling_station_1, verifying station_checks_verified and preparing for loading.  

## Managing Load Slot Constraints  
- Handling_station_1 uses load_slot_3, leaving no slots for handling_station_2, as only three slots (1, 2, 3) are available initially.  
- To process all four entities, authorize some stations early to free slots, as load slots only become available after authorization.

## Processing Station Tasks  
- Handling_station_1 is selected for processing, assigning load_slot_3 and reserving weighing_station_1.  
- Vehicle readiness and container allocation occur, priming the station for further tasks.  

## Managing Handler Assignments  
- Release the handler from receiving_group_1, making it available for handling_station_1.  
- Assign the handler to handling_station_1, enabling veterinary and biosecurity inspections.  

## Conducting Inspections  
- Attach veterinary_kit_1 to handling_station_1, performing inspection and marking vet_completed.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
